revision.text: #REDIRECT[[奈良県護国神社]]

{{神社| 名称=奈良県護国神社| 画像=[[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]]| 所在地=奈良県奈良市古市町1984| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| 祭神=奈良県出身の護国の英霊29,110柱| 創建=昭和17年（1942年）| 社格=内務大臣指定護国神社・別表神社| 本殿=[[流造]]| 例祭=[[4月15日]]・[[10月22日]]}}
'''奈良県護国神社'''（ならけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。[[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]までの国難に殉じた奈良県出身者29,110柱の[[英霊]]を祀る。
昭和14年（[[1939年]]）6月、奈良県知事を会長として護国神社建設奉賛会が組織され、昭和15年10月に創立を許可されて造営を開始、昭和17年（[[1942年]]）に竣工・鎮座し、内務大臣指定護国神社となった。大東亜戦争後のGHQ占領時代、附近の古名よりとった「高円神社（たかまどじんじゃ）」に改称していた。
3月末から4月始めの椿の時期には「椿祭り」が行われる。
{{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:奈良県の神社]] [[Category:奈良市]] [[Category:奈良市の歴史]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]]

'''奈良県護国神社'''（ならけんごこくじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。[[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]までの国難に殉じた奈良県出身者29,110柱を祀る。
昭和14年（[[1939年]]）6月、奈良県知事を会長として護国神社建設奉賛会が組織され、昭和15年10月に創立を許可されて造営を開始、昭和17年（[[1942年]]）に竣工・鎮座し、内務大臣指定護国神社となった。大東亜戦争後のGHQ占領時代、附近の古名よりとった「高円神社（たかまどじんじゃ）」に改称していた。
3月末から4月始めの椿の時期には「椿祭り」が行われる。

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:10 (UTC)}} {{神社| 名称=奈良県護国神社| 画像=[[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]]| 所在地=奈良県奈良市古市町1984| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| 祭神=奈良県出身の国事殉難者
奈良県内の消防団員
29,245柱（2015年現在）境内で頒布されている[[パンフレット]]「奈良県護国神社のご案内（平成二十七年秋補筆）」による。| 創建=昭和17年（1942年）| 社格=[[護国神社|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[別表神社]]| 本殿=[[流造]]| 例祭=[[4月15日]]・[[10月22日]]}} '''奈良県護国神社'''（ならけんごこくじんじゃ、旧字表記：'''奈良縣護國神社'''）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。 ==祭神== [[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]までの国難に殉じた奈良県出身者の[[軍人]]・[[軍属]]・[[従軍看護婦]]、[[満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍]]の殉職者、県内消防団員、合計29,245柱（2015年現在）を祀る。 ==境内== [[奈良盆地]]を一望できる高円山の西麓に位置する。1万5千[[坪]]の面積を誇る境内は「高円の杜」とも称される。 社殿以外には、[[忠魂碑]]、奈良県出身全戦没者のための「戦没者[[慰霊塔]]」、[[ビルマの戦い|ビルマ]]での戦没者の慰める[[パゴダ]]状の「ビルマの塔」、満蒙開拓団青少年義勇軍殉職者の慰霊碑「拓魂碑」、県下消防団殉職者慰霊碑「消防殉職之碑」などの[[石碑]]が建立されている。 1980年（昭和55年）以降、郷土の御霊を慰めるために、[[ツバキ|椿]]が多数樹木されている。3月末から4月始めの椿の時期には「椿祭り」が行われる。 ==沿革== 明治維新後、奈良県では奈良市飛火野の浄地にて祭壇を設置し、戦没者の英霊を招魂して慰霊祭を実施していた。 [[1939年]]（昭和14年）6月、奈良県知事を会長として護国神社建設奉賛会が組織され、昭和15年10月に創立を許可されて造営を開始。 [[1942年]]（昭和17年）9月に完成。同年10月13日、内務大臣指定護国神社となり、社号が「奈良県護国神社」となる。同年11月22日に鎮座祭が行われ、3千柱が祀られた。その後も、大東亜戦争の情勢悪化により戦没者が増え続け、合祀祭神が年毎に増加していった。 [[1947年]]（昭和22年）12月10日、社号を「'''高円神社'''（たかまどじんじゃ）」に改称。 [[1952年]]（昭和27年）7月23日、社号を「'''奈良県護国神社'''」に復す。 [[1966年]]（昭和42年）7月1日、[[神社本庁]][[別表神社]]に加列。 ==祭事== 年間に行われる祭事 *椿祭り - 3月末の土日 *春季大祭 - 4月15日 *戦没者追悼式 - 8月15日 *秋季大祭 - 10月22日 *上記以外に、終戦20週年を迎えた1965年（昭和40年）以降、10年ごとに終戦臨時大祭が行われている。20週年（1965年）と70週年（2015年）は10月22日、それ以外の年は8月15日。 == 近隣施設 == 周辺には[[古墳]]が点在しており、護国神社前池中古墳、古市方形墳がある。 社地の東側に[[山辺の道]]（奈良道）が通っており、境内にはそれについて書かれた看板が設置されている。 == 交通 == *[[奈良交通|奈良交通バス]][[奈良交通奈良営業所|21・55・56・57・58・61・62系統]] 「護国神社」 バス停下車 徒歩3分 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 奈良県護国神社｜奈良市｜奈良県神社検索｜奈良県神道青年会] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 奈良県護国神社 - 山の辺の道「奈良道」を守る会] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:10 (UTC)}} {{神社| 名称=奈良県護国神社| 画像=[[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]]| 所在地=[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]古市町1984| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| 祭神=奈良県出身の国事殉難者
奈良県下の消防団員
合計29,245柱（2015年現在）当社境内で頒布されている[[パンフレット]]「奈良県護国神社のご案内（平成二十七年秋補筆）」| 創建=昭和17年（1942年）| 社格=[[護国神社|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[別表神社]]| 本殿=[[流造]]| 例祭=[[4月15日]]・[[10月22日]]}} '''奈良県護国神社'''（ならけんごこくじんじゃ、旧字表記：'''奈良縣護國神社'''）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。 ==祭神== [[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]までの国難に殉じた奈良県出身者の[[軍人]]・[[軍属]]・[[従軍看護婦]]ならびに[[満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍]]の青少年、県下の[[消防団員]]、合計29,245柱（2015年現在）を祀る。 ==境内== [[奈良盆地]]を一望できる高円山の西麓に位置する。1万5千[[坪]]の面積を誇る境内は「高円の杜（たかまどのもり）」とも称される。 社殿以外には、[[忠魂碑]]、奈良県出身全戦没者のための「戦没者[[慰霊塔]]」、[[ビルマの戦い|ビルマ]]での戦没者の慰める[[パゴダ]]状の「ビルマの塔」、満蒙開拓団青少年義勇軍殉職者の慰霊碑「拓魂碑」、県下消防団殉職者の慰碑「消防殉職之碑」などの[[石碑]]が建立されている。 1980年（昭和55年）以降、郷土の御霊を慰めるために、[[ツバキ|椿]]が多数樹木されている。3月末から4月始めが椿の開花時期となり、それに合わせて毎年3月末の[[週末|土日]]に「椿祭り」が挙行されている。 ==沿革== [[明治維新]]後、奈良県では奈良市飛火野の浄地にて祭壇を設置し、戦没者の英霊を招魂して慰霊祭を実施していた。 [[1939年]]（昭和14年）6月、奈良県知事を会長として護国神社建設奉賛会が組織され、昭和15年10月に創立を許可されて造営を開始。 [[1942年]]（昭和17年）9月に完成。同年10月13日、内務大臣指定護国神社となり、社号が「奈良県護国神社」と定まる。同年11月22日に鎮座祭が行われ、3千柱が祀られた。その後も、大東亜戦争の情勢悪化により戦没者が増え続け、合祀祭神が年毎に増加していった。 [[1947年]]（昭和22年）12月10日、社号を「'''高円神社'''（たかまどじんじゃ）」に改称。 [[1952年]]（昭和27年）7月23日、社号を「'''奈良県護国神社'''」に復す。 [[1966年]]（昭和42年）7月1日、[[神社本庁]][[別表神社]]に加列。 ==祭事== 年間に行われる祭事 *新春祈願祭 - 1月3日 *椿祭り - 3月末の[[週末|土日]] *春季大祭 - 4月15日 *[[大祓|水無月大祓式]] - 6月30日 *戦没者追悼式 - 8月15日 *秋季大祭 - 10月22日 *[[大祓|大祓式]] - 12月31日 *御火焚祭 - 12月31日から翌年1月3日 *上記以外に、終戦20周年を迎えた1965年（昭和40年）以降、10年ごとに終戦臨時大祭が行われている。20周年（1965年）と70周年（2015年）は10月22日、それ以外の年は8月15日に挙行。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 * [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]古市町1984 ;交通アクセス *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[桜井線]] [[京終駅]]より徒歩30分 *[[奈良交通|奈良交通バス]] [[奈良交通奈良営業所|21・55・56・57・58・61・62系統]] 護国神社バス停下車 徒歩3分 *[[自家用自動車|自家用車]]は、隣接されている専用駐車場を利用できる。ただし、社務所で無料駐車券を受け取る必要があるほか、午後5時以降は一切の駐車が禁止されている。違反した場合は所轄の警察署へ連絡されるため注意が必要。 ;周辺 *護国神社前池中古墳 *古市方形墳 *[[山辺の道]] *[[奈良佐保短期大学]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 奈良県護国神社｜奈良市｜奈良県神社検索｜奈良県神道青年会] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 奈良県護国神社 - 山の辺の道「奈良道」を守る会] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:10 (UTC)}} {{神社| 名称=奈良県護国神社| 画像=[[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]]| 所在地=[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]古市町1984| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| 祭神=奈良県出身の国事殉難者
奈良県下の消防団員
合計29,245柱（2015年現在）当社境内で頒布されている[[パンフレット]]「奈良県護国神社のご案内（平成二十七年秋補筆）」| 創建=昭和17年（1942年）| 社格=[[護国神社|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[別表神社]]| 本殿=[[流造]]| 例祭=[[4月15日]]・[[10月22日]]}} '''奈良県護国神社'''（ならけんごこくじんじゃ、旧字表記：'''奈良縣護國神社'''）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）である。 ==祭神== [[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]までの国難に殉じた奈良県出身者の[[軍人]]・[[軍属]]・[[従軍看護婦]]ならびに[[満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍]]の青少年、県下の[[消防団員]]、合計29,245柱（2015年現在）を祀る。 ==境内== [[奈良盆地]]を一望できる高円山の西麓に位置する。1万5千[[坪]]の面積を誇る境内は「高円の杜（たかまどのもり）」とも称される。 社殿以外には、[[忠魂碑]]、奈良県出身全戦没者のための「戦没者[[慰霊塔]]」、[[ビルマの戦い|ビルマ]]での戦没者の慰める[[パゴダ]]状の「ビルマの塔」、満蒙開拓団青少年義勇軍殉職者の慰霊碑「拓魂碑」、県下消防団殉職者の慰碑「消防殉職之碑」などの[[石碑]]が建立されている。 1980年（昭和55年）以降、郷土の御霊を慰めるために、[[ツバキ|椿]]が多数樹木されている。3月末から4月初めが椿の開花時期となり、それに合わせて毎年3月末の[[週末|土日]]に「椿祭り」が挙行されている。 ==沿革== [[明治維新]]後、奈良県では奈良市飛火野の浄地にて祭壇を設置し、戦没者の英霊を招魂して慰霊祭を実施していた。 [[1939年]]（昭和14年）6月、奈良県知事を会長として護国神社建設奉賛会が組織され、昭和15年10月に創立を許可されて造営を開始。 [[1942年]]（昭和17年）9月に完成。同年10月13日、内務大臣指定護国神社となり、社号が「奈良県護国神社」と定まる。同年11月22日に鎮座祭が行われ、3千柱が祀られた。その後も、大東亜戦争の情勢悪化により戦没者が増え続け、合祀祭神が年毎に増加していった。 [[1947年]]（昭和22年）12月10日、社号を「'''高円神社'''（たかまどじんじゃ）」に改称。 [[1952年]]（昭和27年）7月23日、社号を「'''奈良県護国神社'''」に復す。 [[1966年]]（昭和42年）7月1日、[[神社本庁]][[別表神社]]に加列。 ==祭事== 年間に行われる祭事 *新春祈願祭 - 1月3日 *椿祭り - 3月末の[[週末|土日]] *春季大祭 - 4月15日 *[[大祓|水無月大祓式]] - 6月30日 *戦没者追悼式 - 8月15日 *秋季大祭 - 10月22日 *[[大祓|大祓式]] - 12月31日 *御火焚祭 - 12月31日から翌年1月3日 *上記以外に、終戦20周年を迎えた1965年（昭和40年）以降、10年ごとに終戦臨時大祭が行われている。20周年（1965年）と70周年（2015年）は10月22日、それ以外の年は8月15日に挙行。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 * [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]古市町1984 ;交通アクセス *[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[桜井線]] [[京終駅]]より徒歩30分 *[[奈良交通|奈良交通バス]] [[奈良交通奈良営業所|21・55・56・57・58・61・62系統]] 護国神社バス停下車 徒歩3分 *[[自家用自動車|自家用車]]は、隣接されている専用駐車場を利用できる。ただし、社務所で無料駐車券を受け取る必要があるほか、午後5時以降は一切の駐車が禁止されている。違反した場合は所轄の警察署へ連絡されるため注意が必要。 ;周辺 *護国神社前池中古墳 *古市方形墳 *[[山辺の道]] *[[奈良佐保短期大学]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 奈良県護国神社｜奈良市｜奈良県神社検索｜奈良県神道青年会] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 奈良県護国神社 - 山の辺の道「奈良道」を守る会] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]]

[[1942年]]（[[昭和]]17年）9月建立。 * 幣殿 - 1942年（昭和17年）9月建立。 * 拝殿 - 1942年（昭和17年）9月建立。 * 社務所 * 参集所 * 忠魂碑 * 戦没者慰霊塔 - 奈良県出身の全戦没者のための慰霊塔。 * [[ミャンマー|ビルマ]]の塔 - [[ビルマの戦い]]での戦没者を慰める[[パゴダ]]状の塔。 * 拓魂碑 - [[満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍]]殉職者の慰霊碑。 * 消防殉職之碑 - 合計29,245柱（2015年現在）当社境内で頒布されている[[パンフレット]]「奈良県護国神社のご案内（平成二十七年秋補筆）」 | 創建=[[1942年]]（[[昭和]]17年） | 社格=[[護国神社|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[別表神社]] | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[4月15日]]・[[10月22日]] }} '''奈良県護国神社'''（ならけんごこくじんじゃ、旧字表記：'''奈良縣護國神社'''）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。1万5千坪の境内は「高円の杜（たかまどのもり）」とも称される。 == 歴史 == [[明治維新]]後、奈良県では奈良市飛火野の浄地にて祭壇を設置し、戦没者の英霊を招魂して慰霊祭を実施していたが、[[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）6月に、奈良県知事を会長として護国神社建設奉賛会が組織されると、[[1940年]]（昭和15年）10月に神社の創立を許可されて[[奈良盆地]]を一望できる高円山の西麓に造営を開始する。 [[1942年]]（昭和17年）9月に社殿が完成、同年10月13日に[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定護国神社となり、社号が「奈良県護国神社」と定まる。同年11月22日に鎮座祭が行われ、3千柱が祀られた。その後も、[[大東亜戦争]]の情勢悪化により戦没者が増え続け、合祀祭神が年毎に増加していった。 [[1947年]]（昭和22年）12月10日、社号を高円神社（たかまどじんじゃ）に改称するが、[[1952年]]（昭和27年）7月23日に奈良県護国神社に戻している。 [[1966年]]（昭和42年）7月1日に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列されている。 [[1980年]]（昭和55年）以降、郷土の御霊を慰めるために、[[ツバキ|椿]]が多数樹木されている。3月末から4月初めが椿の開花時期となり、それに合わせて毎年3月末の[[週末|土日]]に「椿祭り」が挙行されている。 宮田康弘宮司は、1997年（平成9年）5月に設立された[[日本会議]]の地方組織「日本会議奈良・奈良北支部」の支部長を務めている{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/110810.pdf | title=奈良市議会議員 道端孝治 令和元年度 政務活動日収支報告書 | publisher=奈良市 | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}。 == 祭神 == [[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]までの国難に殉じた奈良県出身者の[[軍人]]・[[軍属]]・[[従軍看護婦]]ならびに[[満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍]]の青少年、県下の[[消防団員]]、合計29,245柱（[[2015年]]現在）を祀る。 == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1942年]]（[[昭和]]17年）9月建立。 * 幣殿 - 1942年（昭和17年）9月建立。 * 拝殿 - 1942年（昭和17年）9月建立。 * 社務所 * 参集所 * 忠魂碑 * 戦没者慰霊塔 - 奈良県出身の全戦没者のための慰霊塔。 * [[ミャンマー|ビルマ]]の塔 - [[ビルマの戦い]]での戦没者を慰める[[パゴダ]]状の塔。 * 拓魂碑 - [[満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍]]殉職者の慰霊碑。 * 消防殉職之碑 - 県下消防団殉職者の慰霊碑。 == 祭事 == 年間に行われる祭事 * 新春祈願祭 - 1月3日 * 椿祭り - 3月末の[[週末|土日]] * 春季大祭 - 4月15日 * [[大祓|水無月大祓式]] - 6月30日 * 戦没者追悼式 - 8月15日 * 秋季大祭 - 10月22日 * [[大祓|大祓式]] - 12月31日 * 御火焚祭 - 12月31日から翌年1月3日 * 上記以外に、終戦20周年を迎えた[[1965年]]（昭和40年）以降、10年ごとに終戦臨時大祭が行われている。20周年（1965年）と70周年（[[2015年]]）は10月22日、それ以外の年は8月15日に挙行。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 * [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]古市町1984 ;交通アクセス * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[桜井線]] [[京終駅]]より徒歩30分 * [[奈良交通|奈良交通バス]] [[奈良交通奈良営業所|21・55・56・57・58・61・62系統]] 護国神社バス停下車 徒歩3分 * [[自家用自動車|自家用車]]は、隣接されている専用駐車場を利用できる。ただし、社務所で無料駐車券を受け取る必要があるほか、午後5時以降は一切の駐車が禁止されている。違反した場合は所轄の警察署へ連絡されるため注意が必要。 ;周辺 * 護国神社前池中古墳 * 古市方形墳 * [[山辺の道]] * [[奈良佐保短期大学]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 奈良県護国神社｜奈良市｜奈良県神社検索｜奈良県神道青年会] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 奈良県護国神社 - 山の辺の道「奈良道」を守る会] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:10 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=Nara Gokoku Shrine | 画像=[[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | 所在地={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | 創建=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 17) | 社格=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | 本殿=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | 例祭=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (ならけんごこくじんじゃ, old orthography: '''奈良縣護國神社''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Bekkaku shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia Festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * Main Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == Festivals == Annual festivals * New Year Prayer Festival - January 3 * Camellia Festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand Festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial Ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand Festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood Festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == Location == ;Address * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Bekkaku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Bekkaku shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia Festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * Main Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == Festivals == Annual festivals * New Year Prayer Festival - January 3 * Camellia Festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand Festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial Ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand Festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood Festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == Location == ;Address * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{神社 | 名称=Nara Gokoku Shrine | 画像=[[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | 所在地={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | 創建=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 17) | 社格=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | 本殿=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | 例祭=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (ならけんごこくじんじゃ, old orthography: '''奈良縣護國神社''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Bekkaku shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia Festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * Main Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == Festivals == Annual festivals * New Year Prayer Festival - January 3 * Camellia Festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand Festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial Ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand Festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood Festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == Location == ;Address * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017年8月28日 (月) 09:10 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称=奈良県護国神社 | 画像=[[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]古市町1984 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=奈良県出身の国事殉難者
奈良県下の消防団員
合計29,245柱（2015年現在）当社境内で頒布されている[[パンフレット]]「奈良県護国神社のご案内（平成二十七年秋補筆）」 | 創建=[[1942年]]（[[昭和]]17年） | 社格=[[護国神社|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[別表神社]] | 本殿=[[流造]] | 例祭=[[4月15日]]・[[10月22日]] | 地図={{Location map|Japan Nara|label=奈良県
護国神社|coordinates= {{coord2|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E}}}} }} '''奈良県護国神社'''（ならけんごこくじんじゃ、旧字表記：'''奈良縣護國神社'''）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]にある[[神社]]（[[護国神社]]）。1万5千坪の境内は「高円の杜（たかまどのもり）」とも称される。 == 歴史 == [[明治維新]]後、奈良県では奈良市飛火野の浄地にて祭壇を設置し、戦没者の英霊を招魂して慰霊祭を実施していたが、[[1939年]]（[[昭和]]14年）6月に、奈良県知事を会長として護国神社建設奉賛会が組織されると、[[1940年]]（昭和15年）10月に神社の創立を許可されて[[奈良盆地]]を一望できる高円山の西麓に造営を開始する。 [[1942年]]（昭和17年）9月に社殿が完成、同年10月13日に[[内務大臣 (日本)|内務大臣]]指定護国神社となり、社号が「奈良県護国神社」と定まる。同年11月22日に鎮座祭が行われ、3千柱が祀られた。その後も、[[大東亜戦争]]の情勢悪化により戦没者が増え続け、合祀祭神が年毎に増加していった。 [[1947年]]（昭和22年）12月10日、社号を高円神社（たかまどじんじゃ）に改称するが、[[1952年]]（昭和27年）7月23日に奈良県護国神社に戻している。 [[1966年]]（昭和42年）7月1日に[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列されている。 [[1980年]]（昭和55年）以降、郷土の御霊を慰めるために、[[ツバキ|椿]]が多数樹木されている。3月末から4月初めが椿の開花時期となり、それに合わせて毎年3月末の[[週末|土日]]に「椿祭り」が挙行されている。 [[2009年]]（平成21年）4月16日に設立された「[[日本会議]]奈良北支部」の代表を、宮田康弘宮司は務めている{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=奈良市議会議員 太田晃司 平成29年度 政務活動日収支報告書 | publisher=奈良市 | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}。 == 祭神 == [[明治維新]]から[[大東亜戦争]]までの国難に殉じた奈良県出身者の[[軍人]]・[[軍属]]・[[従軍看護婦]]ならびに[[満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍]]の青少年、県下の[[消防団員]]、合計29,245柱（[[2015年]]現在）を祀る。 == 境内 == * 本殿 - [[1942年]]（[[昭和]]17年）9月建立。 * 幣殿 - 1942年（昭和17年）9月建立。 * 拝殿 - 1942年（昭和17年）9月建立。 * 社務所 * 参集所 * 忠魂碑 * 戦没者慰霊塔 - 奈良県出身の全戦没者のための慰霊塔。 * [[ミャンマー|ビルマ]]の塔 - [[ビルマの戦い]]での戦没者を慰める[[パゴダ]]状の塔。 * 拓魂碑 - [[満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍]]殉職者の慰霊碑。 * 消防殉職之碑 - 県下消防団殉職者の慰霊碑。 == 祭事 == 年間に行われる祭事 * 新春祈願祭 - 1月3日 * 椿祭り - 3月末の[[週末|土日]] * 春季大祭 - 4月15日 * [[大祓|水無月大祓式]] - 6月30日 * 戦没者追悼式 - 8月15日 * 秋季大祭 - 10月22日 * [[大祓|大祓式]] - 12月31日 * 御火焚祭 - 12月31日から翌年1月3日 * 上記以外に、終戦20周年を迎えた[[1965年]]（昭和40年）以降、10年ごとに終戦臨時大祭が行われている。20周年（1965年）と70周年（[[2015年]]）は10月22日、それ以外の年は8月15日に挙行。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 * [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]古市町1984 ;交通アクセス * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[桜井線]] [[京終駅]]より徒歩30分 * [[奈良交通|奈良交通バス]] [[奈良交通奈良営業所|21・55・56・57・58・61・62系統]] 護国神社バス停下車 徒歩3分 * [[自家用自動車|自家用車]]は、隣接されている専用駐車場を利用できる。ただし、社務所で無料駐車券を受け取る必要があるほか、午後5時以降は一切の駐車が禁止されている。違反した場合は所轄の警察署へ連絡されるため注意が必要。 ;周辺 * 護国神社前池中古墳 * 古市方形墳 * [[山辺の道]] * [[奈良佐保短期大学]] == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ 奈良県護国神社｜奈良市｜奈良県神社検索｜奈良県神道青年会] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ 奈良県護国神社 - 山の辺の道「奈良道」を守る会] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:護国神社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{神社 | 名称=Nara Gokoku Shrine | 画像=[[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | 所在地={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | 創建=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 17) | 社格=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | 本殿=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | 例祭=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社[[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Bekkaku shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia Festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * Main Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == Festivals == Annual festivals * New Year Prayer Festival - January 3 * Camellia Festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand Festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial Ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand Festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood Festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == Location == ;Address * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]]]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{神社 | 名称=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | 所在地={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | 創建=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 17) | 社格=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | 本殿=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | 例祭=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社[[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia Festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * Main Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == Festivals == Annual festivals * New Year Prayer Festival - January 3 * Camellia Festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand Festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial Ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand Festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood Festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == Location == ;Address * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] ]]

Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * Main Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == Festivals == Annual festivals * New Year Prayer Festival - January 3 * Camellia Festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand Festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial Ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand Festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood Festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == Location == ;Address * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine[[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine[[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine[[Category:pages with hiragana sorting]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] ]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{神社 | 名称=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | 所在地={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | 創建=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 17) | 社格=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | 本殿=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | 例祭=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社[[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia Festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * Main Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == Festivals == Annual festivals * New Year Prayer Festival - January 3 * Camellia Festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand Festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial Ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand Festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood Festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == Location == ;Address * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] ]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{神社 | 名称=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | 所在地={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | 創建=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 17) | 社格=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | 本殿=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | 例祭=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社[[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == annual festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;address * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine[[Category:Shinto]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] ]]

Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == annual festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;address * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] ]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{神社 | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | address={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 17) | rank=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | reisai=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社[[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == annual festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;address * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] ]]

A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 17) | rank=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | reisai=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社[[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == annual festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;address * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] ]]

The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == annual festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;address * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]]{{Gokoku Shrines}}[[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:original pages]] [[Category:shinto shrines]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[category:shinto shrines]] ]]
