Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:shinto shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:forcing refresh for Confirmed wikipedia redirects 6]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11\]] [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]]\[[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社[[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:shinto shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:forcing refresh for Confirmed wikipedia redirects 6]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11\]] [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]]\[[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:shinto shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11\]] [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]]\[[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社[[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:shinto shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11\]] [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]]\[[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{Gokoku Shrines}} [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:shinto shrines]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}} [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11\]] [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \[[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]]\[[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category: 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11\]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:奈良縣護國神社]] [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:ならけんここくしんしや}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nara Gokoku Shrine}}

As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=draft}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い|12=draft}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=draft}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓|12=draft}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線|12=draft}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通|12=draft}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. {{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=draft}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い|12=draft}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=draft}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓|12=draft}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線|12=draft}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通|12=draft}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:奈良縣護國神社]] [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] {{citation needed|ならけんここくしんしや}} {{citation needed|Nara Gokoku Shrine}}

Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=draft}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い|12=draft}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=draft}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓|12=draft}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線|12=draft}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通|12=draft}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:奈良縣護國神社]] [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] {{citation needed|ならけんここくしんしや}} {{citation needed|Nara Gokoku Shrine}}

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=draft}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い|12=draft}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=draft}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓|12=draft}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線|12=draft}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通|12=draft}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] }} [[ja:奈良縣護國神社]] [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] {{citation needed|ならけんここくしんしや}} {{citation needed|Nara Gokoku Shrine}}

A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|12=draft|12=draft}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft|12=draft}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft|12=draft}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=draft|12=draft}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=draft|12=draft}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓|12=draft|12=draft}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft|12=draft}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線|12=draft|12=draft}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通|12=draft|12=draft}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|ならけんここくしんしや}} {{citation needed|Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]

The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|ならけんここくしんしや}} {{citation needed|Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:奈良縣護國神社]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] {{translated page|ja|奈良縣護國神社|version=6138021|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|12=draft}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|12=draft}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|12=draft}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|12=draft}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=draft}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い|12=draft}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=draft}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓|12=draft}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|12=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|12=draft}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|12=draft}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線|12=draft}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通|12=draft}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学|12=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|ならけんここくしんしや}} {{citation needed|Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|奈良縣護國神社|version=6138021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] }} [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrine|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|qq=draft}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い|qq=draft}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|qq=draft}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓|qq=draft}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線|qq=draft}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通|qq=draft}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学|qq=draft}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{citation needed|ならけんここくしんしや}} {{citation needed|Nara Gokoku Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|奈良縣護國神社|version=6138021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] }} [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]

The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|qq=}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burma Campaign|qq=}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oharai|qq=}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Line|qq=}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kotsu Bus|qq=}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Saho College|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{translated page|ja|奈良縣護國神社|version=6138021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]

{{出典の明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[1942]] ({{ill|Showa|ja|昭和|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Showa|qq=}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrines|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[April 15]]・[[October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} ({{ill|Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrines|lt=Gokoku Shrine|ja|護国神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gokoku Shrine|qq=}}) located in {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|ja|明治維新|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji Restoration|qq=}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|ja|奈良盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Basin|qq=}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo Shrine|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Beppyo Shrine|qq=}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Association of Shinto Shrines|qq=}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|qq=}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma Campaign|ja|ビルマの戦い|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Burma Campaign|qq=}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Oharai|ja|大祓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oharai|qq=}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|ja|奈良県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Nara|ja|奈良市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara|qq=}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|JR West|ja|西日本旅客鉄道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR West|qq=}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|ja|桜井線|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakurai Line|qq=}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus|ja|奈良交通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Kotsu Bus|qq=}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|ja|奈良佐保短期大学|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara Saho College|qq=}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{translated page|ja|奈良縣護國神社|version=6138021|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nara Gokoku Shrine]] [[ja:奈良縣護國神社]]
