The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|qq=|1=Manshū-Mō Kaitaku Seishonen Giyūgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895653}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the [[:en:Burma campaign|Burma Campaign]]. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Oharai]] - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]] Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu Bus]] Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara Saho College|Nara Saho College]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}}

June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Oharai]] - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]] Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu Bus]] Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara Saho College|Nara Saho College]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[:en:1942|1942]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrines|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[:en:April 15|April 15]]・[[:en:October 22|October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|1=Manshū-Mō Kaitaku Seishonen Giyūgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895653}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the [[:en:Burma campaign|Burma Campaign]]. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Oharai]] - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]] Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu Bus]] Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara Saho College|Nara Saho College]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location=[[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[:en:1942|1942]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrines|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[:en:April 15|April 15]]・[[:en:October 22|October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|1=Manshū-Mō Kaitaku Seishonen Giyūgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895653}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the [[:en:Burma campaign|Burma Campaign]]. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Oharai]] - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]] Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu Bus]] Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara Saho College|Nara Saho College]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[:en:1942|1942]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrines|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[:en:April 15|April 15]]・[[:en:October 22|October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|1=Manshū-Mō Kaitaku Seishonen Giyūgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895653}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the [[:en:Burma campaign|Burma Campaign]]. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Oharai]] - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]] Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu Bus]] Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara Saho College|Nara Saho College]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established=[[:en:1942|1942]] ([[:en:Shōwa era|Showa]] 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrines|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival=[[:en:April 15|April 15]]・[[:en:October 22|October 22]] }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] ([[:en:Gokoku shrines|Gokoku Shrine]]) located in [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]], Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the [[:en:Meiji Restoration|Meiji Restoration]], a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the [[:en:Nara Basin|Nara Basin]]. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a [[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] by the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|1=Manshū-Mō Kaitaku Seishonen Giyūgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895653}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the [[:en:Burma campaign|Burma Campaign]]. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * [[:en:Ōharae-shiki|Oharai]] - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] [[:en:Nara (city)|Nara]] Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|JR West]] [[:en:Sakurai Line|Sakurai Line]] Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from [[:en:Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|Nara Kotsu Bus]] Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara Saho College|Nara Saho College]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ {{draft categories| }} [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * [[:en:Nara Saho College|Nara Saho College]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:28, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Referencesの明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established={{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrines|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival={{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15}}・{{ill|October 22|en|October 22|WD=Q2957|lt=October 22}} }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}}) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|1=Manshū-Mō Kaitaku Seishonen Giyūgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895653}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma campaign|en|Burma campaign|WD=Q221519|lt=Burma Campaign}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|WD=Q11261910|lt=Oharai}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|WD=Q865801|lt=Sakurai Line}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|en|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|WD=Q11445000|lt=Nara Kotsu Bus}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|en|Nara Saho College|WD=Q6965245|lt=Nara Saho College}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|WD=Q865801|lt=Sakurai Line}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|en|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|WD=Q11445000|lt=Nara Kotsu Bus}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|en|Nara Saho College|WD=Q6965245|lt=Nara Saho College}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{Referencesの明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established={{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrines|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival={{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15}}・{{ill|October 22|en|October 22|WD=Q2957|lt=October 22}} }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine}}) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|1=Manshū-Mō Kaitaku Seishonen Giyūgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895653}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma campaign|en|Burma campaign|WD=Q221519|lt=Burma Campaign}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|WD=Q11261910|lt=Oharai}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|WD=Q865801|lt=Sakurai Line}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|en|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|WD=Q11445000|lt=Nara Kotsu Bus}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|en|Nara Saho College|WD=Q6965245|lt=Nara Saho College}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{Referencesの明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established={{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942|1=1942}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrines|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival={{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15|1=April 15}}・{{ill|October 22|en|October 22|WD=Q2957|lt=October 22|1=October 22}} }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|1=Manshū-Mō Kaitaku Seishonen Giyūgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895653}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma campaign|en|Burma campaign|WD=Q221519|lt=Burma Campaign|1=Burma campaign}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|WD=Q11261910|lt=Oharai|1=Ōharae-shiki}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|WD=Q865801|lt=Sakurai Line|1=Sakurai Line}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|en|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|WD=Q11445000|lt=Nara Kotsu Bus|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|en|Nara Saho College|WD=Q6965245|lt=Nara Saho College|1=Nara Saho College}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|WD=Q11261910|lt=Oharai|1=Ōharae-shiki}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|WD=Q865801|lt=Sakurai Line|1=Sakurai Line}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|en|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|WD=Q11445000|lt=Nara Kotsu Bus|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|en|Nara Saho College|WD=Q6965245|lt=Nara Saho College|1=Nara Saho College}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Naraken-Gokoku-jinja]][[ja:奈良県護国神社]] [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{Referencesの明記|date=2017-8-28}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Nara Gokoku Shrine | image = [[File:Nara Gokoku Shrine.jpg|250px]] | location={{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}} Furuichi-cho 1984 | coordinates={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|39|47|N|135|50|35|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=National martyrs from Nara Prefecture
Firefighters from Nara Prefecture
A total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015)Pamphlet "Nara Gokoku Shrine Guide (Updated in Autumn 2015)" distributed on the shrine grounds | established={{ill|1942|en|1942|WD=Q18625|lt=1942|1=1942}} ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Showa|1=Shōwa era}} 17) | type=[[Gokoku Shrines|内務大臣指定護国神社]]・[[Bekkaku shrine|別表神社]] | main hall=[[Nagare-zukuri]] | festival={{ill|April 15|en|April 15|WD=Q2497|lt=April 15|1=April 15}}・{{ill|October 22|en|October 22|WD=Q2957|lt=October 22|1=October 22}} }} '''Nara Gokoku Shrine''' (奈良縣護國神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} ({{ill|Gokoku shrines|en|Gokoku shrines|WD=Q1534477|lt=Gokoku Shrine|1=Gokoku Shrines}}) located in {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}}, {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}, Japan. The 15,000 tsubo precincts are also known as "Takama no Mori" (高円の杜). == History == After the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|WD=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, a ritual altar was set up in the purged area of Tobihino in Nara City to summon and honor the spirits of the war dead. In June 1939 (Showa 14), the Nara Prefecture Governor organized the Gokoku Shrine Construction Support Association, and in October 1940 (Showa 15), the construction of the shrine was approved. Construction began on the western foothills of Mount Takamado, overlooking the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|WD=Q10942269|lt=Nara Basin|1=Nara Basin}}. The shrine buildings were completed in September 1942 (Showa 17), and on October 13 of the same year, the shrine was designated as a Gokoku Shrine by the Minister of Home Affairs and named "Nara Gokoku Shrine." On November 22, 1942, the enshrinement ceremony took place, and 3,000 deities were enshrined. As the Pacific War worsened, the number of war dead continued to increase, and the number of enshrined deities increased annually. On December 10, 1947 (Showa 22), the shrine was renamed "Takamado Shrine" (たかまどじんじゃ), but on July 23, 1952 (Showa 27), it reverted to Nara Gokoku Shrine. On July 1, 1966 (Showa 41), it was listed as a {{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine|1=Beppyo shrine}} by the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|1=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. Since 1980 (Showa 55), many camellias have been planted to comfort the spirits of the homeland. The blooming period of the camellias is from late March to early April, and the "Camellia festival" is held on the last weekend of March every year. Since April 16, 2009 (Heisei 21), Miyata Yasuhiro, the chief priest, has served as the representative of the "Japan Conference Nara North Branch."{{cite web | url=https://www.city.nara.lg.jp/uploaded/attachment/20185.pdf | title=Nara City Council Member Koji Ota Fiscal Year 2017 Political Activity Income and Expenditure Report | publisher=Nara City | date= | accessdate=2024-2-27 }}. == Deities == The shrine enshrines a total of 29,245 deities (as of 2015), including soldiers, military personnel, military nurses, and young men of the {{ill|Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|ja|満蒙開拓青少年義勇軍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army|1=Manshū-Mō Kaitaku Seishonen Giyūgun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895653}}, as well as firefighters from Nara Prefecture, who perished in national crises from the Meiji Restoration to the Pacific War. == Precincts == * main hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Offering Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Worship Hall - Built in September 1942 (Showa 17). * Shrine Office * Assembly Hall * Loyalty Monument * War Dead Memorial Tower - Memorial tower for all war dead from Nara Prefecture. * Burma Tower - A pagoda-style tower to console the spirits of those who perished in the {{ill|Burma campaign|en|Burma campaign|WD=Q221519|lt=Burma Campaign|1=Burma campaign}}. * Takumon Monument - Memorial monument for the Manchurian and Mongolian settlers youth volunteer army who perished. * Firefighter Memorial Monument - Memorial monument for fallen firefighters from Nara Prefecture. == festivals == festivals * New Year Prayer festival - January 3 * Camellia festival - Last weekend of March * Spring Grand festival - April 15 * {{ill|Nagoshi no Harae|ja|水無月大祓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagoshi no Harae|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - June 30 * War Dead Memorial ceremony - August 15 * Autumn Grand festival - October 22 * {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|WD=Q11261910|lt=Oharai|1=Ōharae-shiki}} - December 31 * Firewood festival - December 31 to January 3 * In addition to the above, a special grand festival is held every 10 years since the 20th anniversary of the end of the war in 1965 (Showa 40). The 20th and 70th anniversaries (2015) were held on October 22, while other years were held on August 15. == location == ;location * {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} {{ill|Nara (city)|en|Nara (city)|WD=Q169134|lt=Nara|1=Nara (city)}} Furuichi-cho 1984 ;Transportation * 30 minutes walk from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=JR West|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Sakurai Line|en|Sakurai Line|WD=Q865801|lt=Sakurai Line|1=Sakurai Line}} Kyobate Station * 3 minutes walk from {{ill|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|en|Nara Kotsu Bus Lines|WD=Q11445000|lt=Nara Kotsu Bus|1=Nara Kotsu Bus Lines}} Gokoku Shrine bus stop on the 21, 55, 56, 57, 58, 61, 62 routes * For private cars, a dedicated parking lot is available, but a free parking ticket must be obtained at the shrine office. No parking is allowed after 5 PM. Violations will be reported to the police station. ;Nearby * Gokoku Shrine Mae Ike Chukofun * Furuichi Square Mound * {{ill|Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe-no-Michi|lt=Yamanobe no Michi|ja|山辺の道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanobe no Michi|qq=}} * {{ill|Nara Saho College|en|Nara Saho College|WD=Q6965245|lt=Nara Saho College|1=Nara Saho College}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.narashinsei.com/search/%e3%83%8a%e8%a1%8c/%e5%a5%88%e8%89%af%e7%9c%8c%e8%ad%b7%e5%9b%bd%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be/ Nara Gokoku Shrine | Nara City | Nara Shrine Search | Nara Prefecture Shinto Youth Association] * [https://www.yamanobenara.com/%E5%A5%88%E8%89%AF%E7%9C%8C%E8%AD%B7%E5%9B%BD%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE/ Nara Gokoku Shrine - Yamanobe no Michi "Nara Road" Protection Association] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Gokoku Shrines}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ \ [[ja:奈良県護国神社]] {{translated page|ja|奈良県護国神社|version=102009229|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q11445249}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Gokoku shrines]] [[Category:shinto shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
